message:
fix(backend): _fnx_to_nx handles node_for_adding / u_of_edge / v_of_edge attr collisions (br-yr7kf)

Fresh-eyes review of backend.py::_fnx_to_nx found three more
``**attrs`` splat collision sites of the same class as
franken_networkx-uphdr / -9x7r0.

nx.Graph.add_node's positional parameter is named
``node_for_adding``; nx.Graph.add_edge's are ``u_of_edge`` and
``v_of_edge``. ``_fnx_to_nx`` used the splat pattern
``G.add_node(node, **attrs)`` / ``G.add_edge(u, v, **attrs)`` which
raises ``TypeError: got multiple values for argument ...`` when a
user attribute name collides with any of those three.

Fix: switch to ``add_nodes_from`` with 2-tuples and
``add_edges_from`` with 3-tuples (simple graph) or 4-tuples
(multigraph) so the attr dict goes in positionally and never
touches the colliding parameter name.

_fnx_to_nx is the conversion gateway for every nx-delegation path
(union-with-rename, compose multigraph write, GEXF multigraph
delegation, etc.) so this silently unblocks those chains for
graphs with the affected attr names.

Regression tests parametrized over the three collision names, for
both node and edge paths, plus a combined-collision test on
MultiGraph.

Suite: 3957 passed / 0 failed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -964,6 +964,7 @@

 {"id":"franken_networkx-yqou","title":"[C3] ComplexityWitness type with Merkle decision-path hash","description":"**Track:** C. **Priority:** P0. **Type:** feature.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:23:21.753437351Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:24:16.207699285Z","closed_at":"2026-04-09T00:24:16.207451856Z","close_reason":"duplicate from dry-run double-create","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-yr27","title":"parity: predecessor should support return_seen keyword","description":"predecessor currently rejects NetworkX's return_seen keyword with TypeError('unexpected keyword argument return_seen'). Upstream accepts return_seen=True and returns the BFS depth map alongside predecessor data. Match the full signature and return-shape contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:08:59.862800527Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:48:30.435184474Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:48:30.433347140Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"franken_networkx-yr7kf","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] _fnx_to_nx crashes on node attr 'node_for_adding' or edge attr 'u_of_edge'/'v_of_edge'","description":"Fresh-eyes review of ``python/franken_networkx/backend.py::_fnx_to_nx``
turned up three more ``**attrs`` splat collisions of the same class
as franken_networkx-uphdr / -9x7r0.

nx's ``Graph.add_node`` signature is ``(node_for_adding, **attr)``
and ``Graph.add_edge`` is ``(u_of_edge, v_of_edge, **attr)``.
``_fnx_to_nx`` calls both via the splat pattern:

    G.add_node(node, **node_view[node])
    G.add_edge(u, v, **fg.edges[u, v])

When the attribute dict contains a literal ``'node_for_adding'``
(nodes), ``'u_of_edge'`` (edges), or ``'v_of_edge'`` (edges), splatting
supplies a second value for the positional parameter and raises
``TypeError: Graph.add_node() got multiple values for argument
'node_for_adding'``.

Reproducer::

    import franken_networkx as fnx
    from franken_networkx.backend import _fnx_to_nx

    fg = fnx.Graph(); fg.add_node('a')
    fg.nodes['a']['node_for_adding'] = 'x'
    _fnx_to_nx(fg)   # TypeError

    fg = fnx.Graph(); fg.add_edge('a', 'b', u_of_edge='x')
    _fnx_to_nx(fg)   # TypeError

    fg = fnx.Graph(); fg.add_edge('a', 'b', v_of_edge='x')
    _fnx_to_nx(fg)   # TypeError

Impact:

- ``_fnx_to_nx`` is the conversion helper used by every
  parity-via-nx delegation path (union with rename, compose
  fallback, write_gexf multigraph delegation, etc.). Any graph
  with those attribute names is unconvertible and every downstream
  delegation crashes.
- Attr names are legitimate — a user building a graph to model
  metadata about a graph itself might genuinely pick these names.

Fix: switch to ``add_nodes_from`` with 2-tuples and
``add_edges_from`` with 3-tuples (simple graph) or 4-tuples
(multigraph) so the attr dict is passed positionally and never
collides with the positional parameter name. Same pattern used
in the uphdr fix in c2dda76.

Regression tests in tests/python/test_attribute_access_parity.py
— round-trip an fnx graph with each of the three collision-name
attrs through ``_fnx_to_nx`` and back via
``franken_networkx.readwrite._from_nx_graph``.

Tag: [HIGH] — TypeError with no fallback; blocks every
nx-delegation path for graphs with those attrs. Not a silent
data-loss.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:28:21.391260670Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:28:25.657636204Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Keep solver/dtype/normalization semantics aligned with upstream and extend tests/python/test_centrality_extensions_parity.py with a no-fallback guard.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T01:00:42.639781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T02:36:29.835668260Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T02:36:29.833738667Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Preserve the existing native fast path for supported callables, but match upstream validation/error contracts and add parity coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T01:00:42.638726322Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T01:57:24.761096529Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T01:57:24.759233211Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-yxcz","title":"[G9] Optimal-transport fixture diversity (Wasserstein on graph spectra)","description":"**Track:** G. **Priority:** P3. **Type:** feature.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:23:21.753437351Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:24:16.237497609Z","closed_at":"2026-04-09T00:24:16.237261882Z","close_reason":"duplicate from dry-run double-create","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -380,7 +380,15 @@ def _nx_to_fnx(G):
 
 
 def _fnx_to_nx(fg):
-    """Convert a FrankenNetworkX graph to the matching NetworkX graph type."""
+    """Convert a FrankenNetworkX graph to the matching NetworkX graph type.
+
+    Uses ``add_nodes_from`` / ``add_edges_from`` with attrs passed
+    positionally in the tuple so attr names that collide with nx's
+    positional parameters (``node_for_adding`` on nodes;
+    ``u_of_edge`` / ``v_of_edge`` on edges) don't raise the
+    ``multiple values for argument`` TypeError
+    (franken_networkx-yr7kf, same class as -uphdr / -9x7r0).
+    """
     import networkx as nx
 
     if fg.is_multigraph():
@@ -393,11 +401,10 @@ def _fnx_to_nx(fg):
     else:
         G = nx.Graph()
     node_view = getattr(fg, "nodes", None)
-    for node in fg:
-        if node_view is not None:
-            G.add_node(node, **node_view[node])
-        else:
-            G.add_node(node)
+    if node_view is not None:
+        G.add_nodes_from((node, dict(node_view[node])) for node in fg)
+    else:
+        G.add_nodes_from(fg)
     if fg.is_multigraph():
         seen = set()
         for u in fg:
@@ -407,11 +414,14 @@ def _fnx_to_nx(fg):
                     if edge_id in seen:
                         continue
                     seen.add(edge_id)
-                for key, attrs in keyed_attrs.items():
-                    G.add_edges_from([(u, v, key, dict(attrs))])
+                G.add_edges_from(
+                    (u, v, key, dict(attrs))
+                    for key, attrs in keyed_attrs.items()
+                )
     else:
-        for u, v in fg.edges:
-            G.add_edge(u, v, **fg.edges[u, v])
+        G.add_edges_from(
+            (u, v, dict(fg.edges[u, v])) for u, v in fg.edges
+        )
     G.graph.update(dict(fg.graph))
     return G

@@ -379,3 +379,40 @@ def test_tensor_product_preserves_edge_attribute_named_key():
     # tensor_product should not raise with 'key' attr present
     product = fnx.tensor_product(a, b)
     assert isinstance(product, (fnx.MultiGraph, fnx.Graph))
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# franken_networkx-yr7kf: _fnx_to_nx must handle attr names that collide
+# with nx's add_node / add_edge positional parameters.
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("attr_name", ["node_for_adding", "u_of_edge", "v_of_edge"])
+def test_fnx_to_nx_handles_collision_node_attr(attr_name):
+    from franken_networkx.backend import _fnx_to_nx
+
+    fg = fnx.Graph()
+    fg.add_node("a")
+    fg.nodes["a"][attr_name] = "sentinel"
+    result = _fnx_to_nx(fg)
+    assert dict(result.nodes["a"]) == {attr_name: "sentinel"}
+
+
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("attr_name", ["node_for_adding", "u_of_edge", "v_of_edge"])
+def test_fnx_to_nx_handles_collision_edge_attr(attr_name):
+    from franken_networkx.backend import _fnx_to_nx
+
+    fg = fnx.Graph()
+    fg.add_edge("a", "b", **{attr_name: "sentinel"})
+    result = _fnx_to_nx(fg)
+    assert dict(result["a"]["b"]) == {attr_name: "sentinel"}
+
+
+def test_fnx_to_nx_handles_collision_on_multigraph_edge_attrs():
+    from franken_networkx.backend import _fnx_to_nx
+
+    fg = fnx.MultiGraph()
+    fg.add_edge("a", "b", u_of_edge="x", v_of_edge="y")
+    result = _fnx_to_nx(fg)
+    edges = list(result.edges(keys=True, data=True))
+    assert edges == [("a", "b", 0, {"u_of_edge": "x", "v_of_edge": "y"})]
